title: When to Use a Separate Methodology Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)
summary: Explains why and when a project should maintain a distinct methodology WBS apart from the solution WBS, ensuring each complies with the 100 % rule while supporting different stakeholder needs.
content: Projects can maintain two complementary WBSs: one for the solution deliverables (product, service, or process) and another for project‑methodology deliverables (e.g., risk register, quality plan). This separation helps stakeholders focus on the primary outcome while still tracking necessary management deliverables. The two WBSs can be linked or merged as needed, but each must satisfy the 100% rule within its scope.
can_answer: When should a separate methodology WBS be created?
What are the benefits of using a dual WBS approach?
